perturbation.original_text: 8.1 The initial term of this Agreement shall, unless sooner terminated by consent of all parties, expires in five (5) years from the date of Effective Date. NVOS and HGF may renew the Agreement within two (2) years of the expiry of the initial term upon mutual understanding. 8.2 It is understood that a subsequent renewal of a five (5) year term will be negotiated in good faith and shall carry terms very close to the original Agreement. 8.3 Both parties may enter into buyout negotiations with the other Party on terms agreeable to both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 The initial term of this Agreement shall, unless sooner terminated by consent of all parties, expires in five (5) years from the date of Effective Date. NVOS and HGF may renew the Agreement within two (2) years of the expiry of the initial term upon mutual understanding. 8.2 It is understood that a subsequent renewal shall be for a term determined at the time of renewal and will be based on market conditions at that time and will be negotiated in good faith. 8.3 Both parties may enter into buyout negotiations with the other Party on terms agreeable to both Parties.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text in section 8.2 introduces a contradiction. Initially, renewal terms were 'very close to the original agreement.' Now, renewal terms are based on 'market conditions at that time,' making the renewal terms uncertain and potentially vastly different. This contradicts the original intent of maintaining similar terms and introduces uncertainty in Article 20.1. It's unclear if 'fully develop the Primary Projects as well as all projects associated to this agreement as per this agreement' still applies if the agreement drastically changes upon renewal.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NVOS and HGF covenant and agree to make their best efforts to fully develop the Primary Projects as well as all projects associated to this agreement as per this agreement at all times faithfully, honestly and diligently perform or cause to be performed their obligations hereunder and to continuously exert best efforts to promote and enhance the business and in that regards they hereby covenant and agree, so long as this Agreement shall remain in effect, to operate the business, as to preserve, maintain and enhance the reputation of NVOS and HGF through the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original agreement stipulated renewal terms would be 'very close to the original Agreement.' The changed text allows renewal terms to be based on 'market conditions,' which could drastically alter the agreement's scope and financial implications. This introduces uncertainty and directly conflicts with the requirement in Article 20.1 for both parties to 'fully develop the Primary Projects as well as all projects associated to this agreement as per this agreement.' The change creates an in-text contradiction because if the agreement terms change significantly upon renewal based on market conditions, the original obligation to develop projects 'as per this agreement' becomes unclear and potentially unenforceable, constituting an ambiguity as defined.

perturbation.original_text: 9.3 To remunerate HGF on the basis of thirty percent (30%) of net Company income basis on an annual basis commencing 12 months after the first full 12-month revenue period.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.3 To remunerate HGF on the basis of a percentage of net Company income to be determined annually, with the initial percentage set at thirty percent (30%), subject to adjustments based on performance metrics and contributions, commencing 12 months after the first full 12-month revenue period.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text in section 9.3 makes the remuneration percentage variable. The original text specifies 'thirty percent (30%)' which is straightforward. The revised text indicates it's 'subject to adjustments based on performance metrics and contributions.' This contradicts the definiteness of the initial agreement if later in section 12.1 says 'The distribution will be based on NVOS audited review and will be made within three months of annual considerations on the basis of a seventy percent (70%) of net profit to NVOS and thirty percent (30%) of net profit to HGF'. This creates ambiguity about the real distribution.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The distribution will be based on NVOS audited review and will be made within three months of annual considerations on the basis of a seventy percent (70%) of net profit to NVOS and thirty percent (30%) of net profit to HGF.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in section 9.3 introduces variability in the remuneration percentage, stating it is 'subject to adjustments based on performance metrics and contributions.' This directly contradicts the definite 'thirty percent (30%)' stated in the original text of 9.3, and more importantly the fixed 'thirty percent (30%) of net profit to HGF' in the contradicted text. This creates ambiguity and a clear contradiction regarding the actual distribution percentage, because the Contradicted Text dictates that the distribution will be a fixed 30%.

perturbation.original_text: 11.1 All staffing, including but not limited to, management, specialized or general labor requirements for farming will be the sole responsibility of HGF.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.1 While staffing, including but not limited to, management, specialized or general labor requirements for farming will generally be the responsibility of HGF, NVOS reserves the right to provide personnel for oversight and training purposes at their discretion.
perturbation.explanation: Section 11.1 initially states staffing is the 'sole responsibility of HGF.' The modified version allows NVOS to 'provide personnel for oversight and training purposes.' This directly contradicts the original clause, potentially leading to disputes about control over staffing. Since, section 3.1 says 'The parties will work in a Joint Venture relationship with NVOS providing the development and operation of the project including sales and HGF providing the land, farming expertise, biomass and necessary approvals for the development of the agricultural project.' Now, there's ambiguity about the responsabilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties will work in a Joint Venture relationship with NVOS providing the development and operation of the project including sales and HGF providing the land, farming expertise, biomass and necessary approvals for the development of the agricultural project.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that staffing is the 'sole responsibility of HGF.' The modified version allows NVOS to 'provide personnel for oversight and training purposes.' This directly contradicts the original clause. Section 3.1 states the 'The parties will work in a Joint Venture relationship with NVOS providing the development and operation of the project including sales and HGF providing the land, farming expertise, biomass and necessary approvals for the development of the agricultural project.' The modification introduces ambiguity about the responsibilities initially assigned to HGF, which contradicts the clarity established in the original contract.
